points: Weather is the temporary state of the atmosphere at a specific time and place, primarily occurring in the troposphere (6-18 km altitude).
The troposphere is the densest layer of the atmosphere where life exists and all major weather phenomena take place.
Variations in air temperature cause the troposphere to be thicker at the equator and thinner at the poles.
The five measurable elements of weather are temperature, precipitation, atmospheric pressure, wind, and humidity.
Atmospheric pressure decreases with altitude, leading to lower oxygen levels and the need for acclimatisation in high mountain regions.
Relative humidity determines the rate of evaporation; high humidity slows down cooling and makes the environment feel warmer.
Specialized instruments like the rain gauge, barometer, anemometer, and hygrometer are used to monitor weather patterns systematically.
Meteorology uses data from automated weather stations (AWS) to provide early warnings for disasters like cyclones and heat waves.
Historical weather prediction relied on observing natural clues like animal behavior and plant responses to atmospheric changes.
